problem_addressed: Existing interfaces for LM agents (e.g., Linux shell) are not optimized for complex software engineering tasks, limiting their performance.
core_claim: LM agents significantly benefit from specially-designed interfaces like SWE-agent's agent-computer interface (ACI) for software engineering tasks.
key_contributions: Introduction of SWE-agent with a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) for LM agents
Achieved state-of-the-art performance on SWE-bench (12.5% pass@1) and HumanEvalFix (87.7% pass@1)
novelty: Prior work used general applications (e.g., Linux shell) for LM agents, while SWE-agent introduces a tailored interface with LM-friendly commands and feedback formats.
method_summary: SWE-agent uses a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) with LM-friendly commands and structured feedback to enable autonomous software engineering tasks.
method_components: LM-friendly commands for searching and editing files
key_innovation: The agent-computer interface (ACI) designed with LM-friendly commands and feedback formats that align with LM agents' capabilities.
assumptions: LM agents can effectively utilize structured interfaces
The ACI's design aligns with LM agents' capabilities
theoretical_grounding: Based on HCI studies showing interfaces impact human performance, but no specific proofs provided.
baselines_compared: SWE-agent with GPT-4 on full SWE-bench test set
SWE-agent with Claude 3 Opus on Lite split
headline_result: Not explicitly stated in provided text

results_table.value: create, edit, python appears 156 times at turns 1-3
edit, python, find_file appears 71 times at turns 2-4
python, find_file, open appears 61 times at turns 3-5

title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
